titleStmt.title: Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.24

div.p: To all Holy Mother Church's sons, Alan son of Walter, steward of the king of Scots, [sends] greeting. Let present and future know me to have given, conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God, St Mary and St Benedict, and to the monks of Coupar, one full toft in my burgh of Renfrew beside the churchyard, and one net for salmon in the Clyde; to have and possess in everlasting alms just as the monks of the Cistercian Order freely, quitly, and honourably possess any alms; for the soul of King David, and of King Malcolm, and of Earl Henry, and for the soul of my father and of my mother, and for the salvation of my own soul and of my predecessors'. This alms I, Alan, and my heirs, shall warrant for ever to the monks and to those holding of them. With these as witnesses: the bishop of Glasgow; Prior Osbert of Paisley and the convent; William of Wells, chaplain; Richard, parson of Dundov; William of Paisley; Fulk, clerk; Robert Crock; Gilbert son of Swein; Richard Ruhefd; Wulfbrand; Reginald Mothsune; Eadwulf Waite.
